title: Add a repository list panel to a dashboard

blocks.content: In this milestone, you add a panel to your existing dashboard that displays GitHub repositories for an organization. This visualization helps you monitor repository details, including owner, URL, and forks.

Open your existing dashboard. Click **Add** in the top-right toolbar and select **Visualization**. If you don't see an **Add** button, click **Edit** to enter edit mode first, then click **Add** > **Visualization**.
Click the **data source** dropdown and select your GitHub data source from the list. If you have multiple data sources, look for the one named after your GitHub connection.
If you see a **New query editor** banner with a **Back to classic** button, click **Back to classic**. The GitHub data source requires the classic query editor to show the Query Type dropdown.
In the query editor at the bottom of the screen, find the **Query Type** dropdown and choose **Repositories**.
Enter your GitHub organization name in the **Owner** field. For example, `grafana`.
Enter your repository name in the **Repository** field. For example, `grafana`.
In the **Time Field** dropdown, select **Created At**.

blocks.reftarget: [data-testid='data-testid Panel editor option pane field input Title']
blocks.content: In the **Panel options** on the right, enter a title for your panel in the **Title** field. For example, `GitHub Complete Repository Overview`.
Click the **time picker** and select an appropriate range, for example **Last 30 days**.
Click **Save dashboard**. In the dialog that appears, click **Save**.
Your dashboard now includes three panels: issues, pull requests, and a repository overview. You can monitor forks, creation date, and owner details across your organization.
